prompt: Create a RideCircle Carpool Marketplace platform that lets passengers and drivers coordinate local carpools entirely offline within a private organization or community. Regular Users can register, log in, and post trip requests with pickup and drop-off text addresses, rider count (1-6), preferred vehicle type (sedan/SUV/van), baggage notes, and a time window (start/end, 12-hour clock). Drivers can browse open requests, search by keywords across titles, descriptions, and tags, and sort by newest, most discussed, or most popular; results highlight matched terms and allow filters such as vehicle type and rider count. A listing workflow supports creating, editing, saving drafts, publishing/unpublishing, and bulk closing outdated requests, with a clear status badge (active, matched, in progress, completed, canceled, disputed) and a visible version history so users can review changes before re-publishing. After a driver accepts, the system generates an order and guides both parties through a simple lifecycle UI: pending match, accepted, in progress, completed, canceled, or in dispute, with inline feedback when actions are blocked. A ratings and UGC experience allows star ratings (1-5), written reviews up to 1,000 characters, and optional photo/video uploads with preview, file size limits (photos up to 5 MB each, videos up to 50 MB, max 5 files per review). Search usability is strengthened with keyword suggestions, recent search history on-device, and a did-you-mean prompt for common typos, plus a no-results fallback showing recently active listings.The backend uses ThinkPHP to implement a decoupled REST-style API consumed by the Layui frontend over the local network or the same machine, exchanging JSON and enforcing consistent validation and error handling. MySQL persists users, organizations, roles/permissions, listings, orders, media records, reviews, moderation actions, and behavior events. Permissions apply at resource granularity for listings, orders, reviews, moderation queue actions, governance dashboards, audit logs, organization settings, and API access, and support both session login and long-lived API tokens with rotation and a default 90-day expiry. Order state transitions are strictly validated: pending match auto-expires after 30 minutes with no driver acceptance; cancellations are allowed free within 5 minutes of acceptance, then require a reason code, and are blocked once marked in progress; disputes can be opened within 72 hours after completion and must be resolved by an Administrator. Content moderation is fully local and rule-based: sensitive-word matching, rate limits (max 3 reviews per user per hour), duplicate detection via text similarity and file fingerprints, and a credibility score that weights reviews by account age (higher after 14 days), completion history, and abnormal patterns; flagged items go to a manual queue for Moderators. Media is stored on local disk with signed, time-limited access URLs (default 10 minutes) and optional watermark overlays on images; hotlink protection is enforced by validating signatures and referrers within the offline domain. Data governance captures browse/click/favorite/rate events and listing metadata, runs nightly deduplication and missing-value completion, records lineage for each cleanup step, and produces quality metrics viewable by Administrators. Sensitive fields such as password hashes and token secrets are stored securely, and logs mask identifiers except for authorized audit users.
